optionA.text: Ventral cirri absent

optionB.text: Ventral cirri present

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri on first setiger only

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri on most setigers

optionA.text: All setae simple, occipital flap present

optionB.text: At least some setae composite, occipital flap absent

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri cylindrical

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri clavate or foliose

optionA.text: Bayonet-setae thick-shafted; segmental ciliary bands absent

optionB.text: Bayonet-setae thin-shafted; ciliary bands present on every segment

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx unarmed

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with teeth

optionA.text: With projecting nuchal lobes, rather than flattened nuchal epaulettes

optionB.text: Nuchal organs flattened epaulettes

optionA.text: Antennae and dorsal cirri clavate

optionB.text: Antennae cylindrical, dorsal cirri flattened

optionA.text: Small forms (usually less than 8 mm); palps fused for at least half their lengths

optionB.text: Larger forms, palps, if fused at all, only fused basally

optionA.text: Palps fused at base; dorsal cirri smooth or irregularly wrinkled

optionB.text: Palpi free to base or absent; dorsal cirri articulated

optionA.text: Antennae, tentacular and dorsal cirri absent

optionB.text: At least tentacular cirri present, usually also antennae and dorsal cirri

optionA.text: Two pairs of tentacular cirri present

optionB.text: One pair of tentacular cirri present

optionA.text: Antennae absent

optionB.text: Three antennae present

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx non-muscular and sinuous

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx at least partially muscular, straight

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri long and filiform

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri globular

optionA.text: Body with dorsal globular papillae in addition to the dorsal cirri

optionB.text: Body without dorsal globular papillae

optionA.text: One antenna present; dorsal cirri rudimentary

optionB.text: Three antennae present; dorsal cirri well developed if sometimes short

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri long and slender

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri short, ovoid or pyriform

optionA.text: Parapodia with erect, contractile dorsal lobes

optionB.text: Parapodia without dorsal lobes

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx unarmed

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with a single anterior tooth

optionA.text: Ventral cirri fused to lower edge of parapodia

optionB.text: Ventral cirri free from parapodia

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri pyriform (flask-shaped)

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri papilliform or ovoid

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx unarmed

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx either with a single tooth or with a circlet of smaller teeth

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with a single large tooth

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with a series of teeth

optionA.text: Dorsum covered with small papillae

optionB.text: Dorsum without papillae

optionA.text: Pharynx with an internal valve

optionB.text: Pharynx without an internal valve

optionA.text: Tentacular cirri absent; dorsal cirri rudimentary

optionB.text: Tentacular cirri present; dorsal cirri well developed

optionA.text: Antennae and tentacular cirri very short

optionB.text: Antennae and tentacular cirri not noticeably short

optionA.text: Enlarged knobbed acicula present in anterior parapodia

optionB.text: Enlarged knobbed acicula absent

optionA.text: Notacicula present

optionB.text: Notacicula absent

optionA.text: Antennae and dorsal cirri absent

optionB.text: Antennae and dorsal cirri present

optionA.text: Three first segments fused with six pairs of tentacular cirri

optionB.text: Maximally two pairs of tentacular cirri on one segment

optionA.text: One pair of tentacular cirri present

optionB.text: Two pairs of tentacular cirri present

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri flattened

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri cylindrical

optionA.text: Composite setae spinigerous

optionB.text: Composite setae falcigerous

optionA.text: Tentacular and dorsal cirri very large, ovoid and inflated

optionB.text: Tentacular and dorsal cirri more or less cylindrical

optionA.text: Middorsal tooth situated posterior in the eversible pharynx

optionB.text: Middorsal tooth situated anterior in the eversible pharynx

optionA.text: Anterior margin of eversible pharynx denticulated

optionB.text: Anterior margin of eversible pharynx smooth

optionA.text: Median body-region with furcate, thick simple setae

optionB.text: Median body-region with composite falcigers

optionA.text: Parapodia long, palps twice as long as prostomium

optionB.text: Parapodia short and conical; palps maximally as long as prostomium

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with a series of very small teeth

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with a limited number of large teeth

optionA.text: Body short, with few segments; large nuchal epaulettes present

optionB.text: Body longer, with numerous segments; nuchal epaulettes absent

optionA.text: Palps absent; first segment with parapodia and setae

optionB.text: Palps present, first segment without parapodia and setae; usually with tentacular cirri

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with a single tooth, a trepan or both

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx unarmed

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with a trepan of several teeth; sometimes with an additional larger single tooth as well

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with a single tooth

optionA.text: Dorsum covered with small papillae

optionB.text: Dorsum smooth

optionA.text: Parapodia with long, digitate distal lobes

optionB.text: Parapodia without distal lobes

optionA.text: Paired, posteriorly directed nuchal lappets present

optionB.text: Nuchal lappets absent

optionA.text: Palpi as long as prostomium; body cylindrical

optionB.text: Palpi small and conical; body flattened

optionA.text: Middorsal tooth attached posteriorly

optionB.text: Middorsal tooth attached anteriorly

optionA.text: Setae simple, distally furcate or subdistally bossed

optionB.text: At least some setae composite

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri in posterior region alternately long and slender or large, bulbously fusiform

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri may alternate between long and short, but all are of similar thickness

optionA.text: Tentacular and dorsal cirri with very few articles; dorsal cirri absent from second segment

optionB.text: Tentacular and dorsal cirri with at least five articles; dorsal cirri present on second segment

optionA.text: Parapodia with pseudocomposite and simple setae in addition to the composite ones

optionB.text: Parapodia maximally with two simple setae in addition to the composite ones

optionA.text: Both composite spinigers and falcigers present

optionB.text: Only composite falcigers present
